title: Break-Even Analysis Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate the exact unit sales volume needed to cover fixed and variable costs, instantly in your browser.
shortDescription: Calculate break-even units and revenue from fixed costs, price, and variable cost per unit.

guideSections.heading: Why Contribution Margin, Not Profit Margin, Drives This Number
guideSections.body: Break-even analysis cares about how much each additional unit contributes toward covering fixed costs — price minus variable cost per unit — not the fully-loaded profit margin reported on a P&L. Confusing the two leads to a break-even estimate that's wrong in either direction depending on how overhead is allocated.

guideSections.heading: Why This Number Shifts Every Time Fixed Costs Change
guideSections.body: Break-even point isn't a fixed property of a product — it moves whenever fixed costs change, such as adding headcount or renewing a software contract. Treating a break-even calculation as a one-time exercise rather than something to revisit after cost structure changes is a common planning gap.
